title: Baccaurea costulata
intro: Baccaurea costulata, also known as the Burmese plum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. The health benefits of Baccaurea costulata include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Baccaurea costulata is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Baccaurea costulata is classified in the family Phyllanthaceae and the order Malpighiales. This species belongs to the genus Baccaurea, which is a group of trees native to Southeast Asia. The family Phyllanthaceae is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the Phyllanthus family. Baccaurea costulata is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is often found in shaded areas. It is a tree species that is known for its unique characteristics and medicinal properties.
